mission.title: Prune Stale Kubeconfig Contexts
mission.description: Safely clean up your kubeconfig by removing contexts for clusters that no longer exist. Creates a timestamped backup first, tests each context, shows you exactly what will be removed, and asks for confirmation before making any changes. After pruning, links to the Cluster Admin dashboard to verify results.

steps.title: Back up your kubeconfig
steps.description:
Create a timestamped backup of your kubeconfig before making any changes.

```bash
BACKUP_PATH="${HOME}/.kube/config.backup-$(date +%Y-%m-%d-%H%M%S)"
cp ~/.kube/config "$BACKUP_PATH"
echo "Backup saved to: $BACKUP_PATH"
ls -la "$BACKUP_PATH"
```

> **IMPORTANT:** Tell the user the exact backup file path so they can restore it if needed.
> Example: "Your kubeconfig has been backed up to ~/.kube/config.backup-2026-03-16-214500"

steps.title: List all kubeconfig contexts and test reachability
steps.description:
Test each context by attempting a lightweight API call. Mark each as reachable or unreachable.

```bash
echo "Testing all kubeconfig contexts..."
echo ""
for ctx in $(kubectl config get-contexts -o name 2>/dev/null); do
  if kubectl --context="$ctx" get namespaces --no-headers -o name 2>/dev/null | head -1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  ✅ $ctx — reachable"
  else
    echo "  ❌ $ctx — UNREACHABLE (stale)"
  fi
done
```

> This tests every context with a 5-second timeout. Unreachable contexts are candidates for removal.
commands: for ctx in $(kubectl config get-contexts -o name 2>/dev/null); do if timeout 5 kubectl --context="$ctx" get namespaces --no-headers -o name 2>/dev/null | head -1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "  ✅ $ctx — reachable"; else echo "  ❌ $ctx — UNREACHABLE"; fi; done

steps.title: Show the user what will be removed and ask for confirmation
steps.description:
Present the list of stale contexts to the user and ask for explicit confirmation before proceeding.

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT proceed without user confirmation. Show them:
1. Which contexts will be removed (only unreachable ones)
2. Which contexts will be kept (reachable ones)
3. The backup file location (from step 1)
4. Ask: "Shall I remove these stale contexts? (yes/no)"

If the user says no, stop here. Their kubeconfig is unchanged.

steps.title: Remove stale contexts (only after user confirmation)
steps.description:
For each unreachable context, remove the context, cluster, and user entries from kubeconfig.

```bash
# For each stale context:
kubectl config delete-context <context-name>
kubectl config delete-cluster <cluster-name>
kubectl config delete-user <user-name>
```

> Only remove contexts that the user confirmed. Each `delete-context` also reports what was removed.
commands: # Replace <stale-ctx> with each confirmed stale context name

steps.title: Verify the cleaned kubeconfig
steps.description:
Show the remaining contexts and confirm the kubeconfig is valid.

```bash
echo "Remaining contexts:"
kubectl config get-contexts
echo ""
echo "Testing current context..."
kubectl cluster-info 2>/dev/null || echo "No active context (expected if all clusters were stale)"
```

steps.title: Open Cluster Admin dashboard to verify
steps.description:
Direct the user to the Cluster Admin dashboard to see the updated cluster list.

**Tell the user:**
"Your kubeconfig has been pruned. Visit the **Cluster Admin** dashboard to verify the updated cluster list:"

- Local: http://localhost:8080/cluster-admin
- Production: https://console.kubestellar.io/cluster-admin

> The stale contexts should no longer appear. If you need to restore, your backup is at the path shown in step 1.

troubleshooting.title: Accidentally removed a valid context
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** A cluster may have been temporarily unreachable during the test.

**Fix:** Restore from the backup created in step 1:
```bash
cp ~/.kube/config.backup-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS ~/.kube/config
```
Replace the timestamp with the actual backup file name shown in step 1.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl config delete-context fails
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The context may have already been removed or the name contains special characters.

**Fix:** Try removing it directly:
```bash
kubectl config unset contexts.<context-name>
```

resolution.summary: Your kubeconfig has been pruned of stale contexts. A backup was created before any changes. Visit the Cluster Admin dashboard to verify the updated cluster list.

prerequisites.resources: None — this operates on local kubeconfig only
prerequisites.description: kubectl must be installed and ~/.kube/config must exist.
